knownIssues.title: Lighthouse tracking requires the External Tracking Faceplate
knownIssues.description: The Cosmos Elite ships with the External Tracking Faceplate pre-installed for Lighthouse 1.0/2.0 tracking. Without this faceplate, the headset falls back to inside-out camera tracking which is inferior. The faceplate disables the inside-out cameras.
knownIssues.workaround: Ensure the External Tracking Faceplate is securely attached before use. If using inside-out mode, swap to the standard Cosmos faceplate. Keep both faceplates stored safely.

knownIssues.title: Link Box can cause intermittent disconnects
knownIssues.description: The HTC Link Box converts the headset tether to DisplayPort and USB and is a known failure point. Random disconnects, audio dropouts, or display flickering may occur.
knownIssues.workaround: Update Link Box firmware via SteamVR. Ensure the Link Box is on a stable surface with good airflow. Replace if disconnects persist — third-party alternatives are available.

knownIssues.title: Cosmos Elite controllers have an unusual ring-less design
knownIssues.description: Unlike Vive Wands or Index Controllers, Cosmos Elite controllers do not have tracking rings. The SteamVR tracking hardware is embedded in the controller body, which some users find less ergonomic.
knownIssues.workaround: Try both the 'comfort grip' and standard grip positions. Third-party grips and accessories are available for improved ergonomics.

optimizationTips.tip: Use SteamVR motion smoothing at 45fps for demanding titles
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Turn on SteamVR motion smoothing so the headset stays comfortable when games are too demanding to run at full speed.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: SteamVR motion smoothing halves the GPU frame rate requirement by synthesizing intermediate frames. At 90Hz, targeting 45fps with motion smoothing enabled is often a better experience than running at 90fps with reduced quality settings. Especially effective in sim titles (DCS, iRacing) on mid-range GPUs.

optimizationTips.tip: 1.2x supersampling noticeably improves sharpness through the optics
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Slightly turning up the picture quality makes a visible difference in sharpness on the Cosmos Elite.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: The Cosmos Elite's 1440x1700 per eye resolution benefits from supersampling to 1.2-1.3x in SteamVR to reduce aliasing through the aspheric lens array. GTX 1080+ class GPUs handle this comfortably at 90Hz in most titles. Set per-application in SteamVR Video settings.

optimizationTips.tip: Run SteamVR in Direct Display Mode for minimum latency
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Direct mode bypasses Windows desktop rendering, reducing the time between head movement and what you see.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: SteamVR Direct Display Mode bypasses the Windows DWM compositor, eliminating 1-2 frames of latency. The Cosmos Elite should not appear as a monitor in Windows Display Settings when correctly configured. Verify in SteamVR → Settings → Developer. May require toggling if the headset appears as a monitor after firmware updates.
